---
title: "Day 1 - Block 3"
subtitle: "Context"
format:
  revealjs:
    footer: "TI Workshop | D1 B3"
    bibliography: ../../../resources/workshop-citations.bib
    embed-resources: true
---

## Context decides output {.center}

::: {.r-fit-text}
Structure beats clever wording.
:::

## Objective

- Turn weak prompts into specs
- Apply instruction hierarchy cleanly
- Compare A/B with one rubric

## Inputs

- One weak domain prompt
- One source pack: table/memo/transcript
- Fixed settings for A/B runs

## What models care about

- First/last prompt zones dominate
- Structure beats clever wording
- Keep constraints explicit and testable

## Instruction hierarchy

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
  A[Role and objective] --> B[Task and scope]
  B --> C[Constraints and evidence rules]
  C --> D[Output schema]
  D --> E[Stop rule and self-check]
```

## Reusable prompting techniques

- Few-shot sets target distribution
- Chain-of-thought decomposes tasks
- Meta-prompting templates structure

## Weak to strong rewrite pattern

```text
Weak: "Read this earnings call and give insights."

Stronger:
Role: Research assistant.
Task: Code statements into labels: demand, pricing, costs, guidance, risk.
Constraints:
1) Use exact quotes only.
2) If absent, return "not found".
3) No causal claims without textual support.
Output: markdown table (label | quote | confidence_1_to_5 | analyst_note)
Quality check: final line = "main uncertainty: ..."
```

## A/B method (one-variable changes)

| Round | Variant A | Variant B | Hold constant |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | no role line | role line added | data, settings |
| 2 | free output | fixed schema | data, settings |
| 3 | no check | self-check line | data, settings |

## Hands-on (E2) {background-color="black"}

- Rewrite one weak prompt
- Run three fair A/B comparisons
- Submit pair + A/B table: `/docs/live-exercises?exercise=E2`

## Registry references {.smaller}

- Prompting + personas: R005, R030
- In-context behavior: R032
- Foundation context: R031

## Common failure modes

- Too many variables changed
- Constraints conflict internally
- Failure explanation stays vague

## Debrief

- What assumption failed first?
- What evidence would falsify output?
- What should not be delegated?
